name: all-fg-go
description: Foreground-sequential fan-out trigger — user says "all fg go" (or equivalent) → print a plan table (branch · subagent_type · iso · goal) then run the prior turn's branches ONE AT A TIME in the foreground, awaiting each (run_in_background:false) with visible `▶ i/N → ✅/⚠/❌` progress, HALTING on the first failure (report + un-run tail). REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered — the foreground-sequential sibling of `all-bg-go` (parallel background). NO parallelism, NO background Agent fan-out — use when each branch must be watched in order (careful review · single-threaded shared resource · debugging). For a self-generating repeatable foreground loop, use `/cycle-fg` instead. Ships `/all-fg-go` + `/afg` 3-char alias.
